title: Generative Audio-Reactive SVG Mandala
description: A creative studio tool that generates rotational-symmetry SVG mandalas from mouse/touch drawing, with real-time audio reactivity via Web Audio API FFT analysis driving stroke width, color interpolation, and scale transformations.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: Trigonometric rotation matrix engine: for each drawn segment, N-fold symmetry replicates paths at θ = i × 2π/N using Math.cos/sin with optional mirror reflection across sector axes
Web Audio API asynchronous microphone capture via getUserMedia, routed through AnalyserNode with configurable FFT 256-bin resolution and smoothing time constant 0.8
Real-time frequency mapping: sub-bass (bins 0-5) drives center scale, mids (bins 6-19) modulate stroke width, highs (bins 20-39) cycle color palette with lerp interpolation
SVG DOM node injection: each replicated segment drawn as a native <line> element with stroke, stroke-width, opacity, and stroke-linecap attributes
Fallback synthetic oscillator generating sine-wave frequency data when no microphone is available, ensuring full testability
Native SVG file exporter: XMLSerializer().serializeToString() builds a valid standalone .svg document with download via Blob URL
Four themed color palettes: Cyan→Magenta, Green→Gold, Purple→Pink, Monochrome with real-time audio-reactive hue mixing
Adjustable symmetry (2-24 slices), stroke width (0.5-6), mirror toggle, and auto-pruning of excess paths to maintain performance
